tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible impaired kidney function. Urine analysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising suspicion for renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, tubular dysfunction) and possible dehydration. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—may need to rule out analytical error or hemoglobinopathy. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and clinical correlation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
